campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. They also discussed extending a temporary rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth recreation scholarships
City Council extends temporary ordinance #2326
Plans advance for bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application being submitted for bike/pedestrian safety project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing where residents could voice concerns about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance (a new city rule) and skipping the first formal reading to speed up the process. The council directed staff to prepare the ordinance for final approval at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held - residents given opportunity to comment
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Details of the specific ordinance topic not specified in agenda

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily focused on administrative housekeeping and approving a construction project, though the specific details of the construction work were not fully listed in the available information.
keyTopics: Approval of March 2026 meeting minutes (multiple sessions)
Authorization of construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Administrative and procedural matters

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several updates to city contracts and projects. Key decisions included approving a new agreement with Vanderlande Industries for airport baggage handling equipment, addressing affordable housing protections when rental subsidy programs end, and approving planning work for the city's storm sewer system upgrades.
keyTopics: Airport baggage system contract update with Vanderlande Industries
Affordable housing protections when subsidy contracts expire
Storm sewer system city-wide master planning
FEMA San José Family Camp project actions
Routine council committee minutes approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including budgets for infrastructure projects and improvements. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement projects and city infrastructure spending
Teen Month proclamation recognizing young residents
National Volunteer Week recognition for community volunteers

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The council opened with a flag salute and highlighted these two important observances as part of their special order of business.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to help residents learn about OpenGov, a tool that shows how Cupertino spends and plans its money. The meeting also covered rules requiring people who lobby the city to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and community training
City budget planning and public engagement
New lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
How to influence city decisions (official process)

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents about challenging city decisions. If you disagree with a City Council decision, you have 90 days from when the decision becomes final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge—after that deadline, you lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over council decisions
Legal rights for residents who disagree with city rulings
California state law requirements for court challenges
How to protect your legal interests after council meetings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's five-year housing and community development plan and new project requests from council members. The council also discussed changes to how council vacancies are filled and reviewed upcoming meeting schedules.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to the council vacancy ordinance
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner wants to officially recognize the building as a historic landmark and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of new space as part of seismic safety and historic restoration work.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade (Category 4 to Category 2)
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area approval for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
